troubleshooting.title: Resolve a NLTK LookupError on first run
troubleshooting.shortdesc: A LookupError: Resource punkt_tab not found error means the NLTK language data required by sentence-count or part-of-speech rules is not installed on your system.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Fix the NLTK LookupError that occurs when running sentence-count or part-of-speech validation rules without the required NLTK language data.

condition.title: Situation
condition.p: You run md-validate validate and see an error similar to:
condition.codeblock: LookupError: Resource punkt_tab not found. Please use the NLTK Downloader to obtain the resource: >>> import nltk; nltk.download('punkt_tab')
condition.p: This error occurs when a rule uses the s (sentence count) or p<N> (part of speech) operators and the required NLTK corpora are not installed.

cause.title: Cause
cause.p: The NLTK language data files (punkt_tab and averaged_perceptron_tagger_eng) are not included in the markdown-validator pip package. They must be downloaded once per Python environment before sentence or POS rules can run.

step.cmd: Activate the virtual environment where markdown-validator is installed.

step.stepresult: NLTK downloads the files to your NLTK data directory (~/nltk_data on macOS/Linux).

cause.title: Cause (CI environment)
cause.p: The CI pipeline does not include an NLTK download step.

steps-informal.p: Add the NLTK download step to your CI workflow before the validation step. See Add markdown-validator to a GitHub Actions workflow for a complete example that includes this step.
